stem: Square P has a side length of x inches. Square Q has a perimeter that is 120 inches greater than the perimeter of square P. The function f gives the area of square Q, in square inches. Which of the following defines f?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. Square P has perimeter 4x, so square Q has perimeter 4x + 120. Then square Q's side is (4x + 120)/4 = x + 30, and its area is (x + 30)².

Choice A uses the perimeter difference as the side increase. Choices C and D mistakenly multiply x by 120.
